meta.title: Portolan — Publish geospatial data without the portal
meta.description: Portolan is a toolkit and set of conventions for publishing geospatial data as cloud-native files on object storage. STAC + GeoParquet + COG, done right.

hero.title: Publish geospatial data
hero.description: Portolan is a toolkit and set of conventions for publishing geospatial data as cloud-native files on object storage. STAC + GeoParquet + COG, done right, for governments and open data publishers.

open.title: Open
open.description: 100% open source, open formats. Apache-2.0 license, open governance, open specs. If Portolan disappeared tomorrow, your data would still work everywhere.

aiReady.title: AI-ready
aiReady.description: STAC metadata is structured for retrieval. Catalogs are queryable by LLM agents and ready for semantic search — not just humans clicking through a portal.

cheap.title: Cheap
cheap.description: Pay only for storage and egress. No servers to run, no database to license, no portal seats to buy. A small government catalog: dollars a month.

sovereign.title: Sovereign
sovereign.description: Your data lives in your bucket, on your cloud, in your jurisdiction. Host on AWS, GCS, Azure, MinIO, Cloudflare R2 — anywhere S3-compatible.

scales.title: Scales to petabytes
scales.description: Cloud object storage handles the scale. Cloud-native formats serve range requests — a 100 GB COG opens in ~80ms, faster than most tile servers.

breaks.title: Breaks the GIS silo
breaks.description: Query with DuckDB, Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, Pandas — not just GIS tools. Every layer is a file at a stable URL, same bytes for everyone.

howItWorks.title: Files on storage, not servers.
howItWorks.subtitle: Portolan replaces traditional SDI servers with cloud-native files on object storage. No databases, no tile servers, no portal software — just files that browsers and AI agents read directly.

convert.title: Convert
convert.description: Transform shapefiles, GeoTIFFs, and other formats into cloud-native equivalents — GeoParquet for vectors, COG for rasters, PMTiles for map tiles.

catalog.title: Catalog
catalog.description: Generate a STAC catalog that describes every asset with structured metadata, rich descriptions, and links that AI agents and tools can follow.

publish.title: Publish
publish.description: Push files to any S3-compatible object storage — AWS, GCS, Azure, R2, MinIO, or Source Cooperative for free open data hosting.

browse.title: Browse
browse.description: Data is instantly accessible through any STAC-compatible viewer, queryable via DuckDB, and ready for AI agents — no servers to run.

cli.name: portolan-cli
cli.title: The CLI
cli.description: Convert, validate, and sync. One command from a folder of shapefiles to a live STAC catalog on S3.
cli.compatibility: Works with AWS · GCS · Azure · R2 · MinIO · Source.coop

viewer.name: portolan-viewer
viewer.title: Browser-native viewer
viewer.description: A lightweight viewer that reads STAC + cloud-native files directly.

skills.name: portolan-skills
skills.title: AI agent skills
skills.description: Ready-made skills that let Claude, Gemini, and Codex agents publish catalogs and query cloud-native geospatial data.

quickstart.title: Quickstart
quickstart.intro: Get started with Portolan in minutes. Browse existing catalogs, then publish your own data.

browse.title: Browse a catalog
browse.description: Portolan catalogs are live on the web. Open the Portolan Browser to explore published datasets — view metadata, preview maps, and inspect data tables. Everything you see is served directly from cloud-native files on object storage, with zero servers.

duckdb.title: Query with DuckDB
duckdb.description: Any GeoParquet file in a Portolan catalog can be queried directly with DuckDB — no download needed. Just point at the URL.

publish.title: Publish your data
publish.description: Two paths to get your data into a Portolan catalog.

cli.title: With the CLI
cli.description: Install the portolan-cli, then init, add, convert, and push.

steps.init: Initialize a new catalog in your data directory
steps.add: Add your data files to the catalog

claude.title: With Claude
claude.description: If you have Claude Code, install the Portolan skill and point Claude at your data. It handles format conversion, metadata generation, and publishing.

steps.run: Then ask Claude to publish your data

registry.title: Browse {count} catalogs
registry.description: Discover and connect to Portolan-compatible STAC catalogs from around the world.

cta.title: Have a catalog to share?
cta.description: Submit your Portolan-compatible catalog to the registry.

submit.title: Submit a catalog
submit.successDescription: Your catalog will appear after review.

map.infoBody: Basemap © CARTO, © OpenStreetMap contributors. Search powered by Nominatim. Catalogs appear as dots; zoom in to see their coverage areas.
map.noLocation: {count, plural, one {# catalog has no location data} other {# catalogs have no location data}}
